West Seattle Freeway, 36th Ave SE to I-5 : environmental impact statement.

The West Seattle Freeway project aims to improve a crucial transportation link between West Seattle and the rest of Seattle by upgrading the existing Spokane Street corridor. The plan includes constructing high-level bridges to facilitate better traffic flow and accommodate larger ships in the Duwamish River. This development is expected to enhance industrial growth in the area while minimizing environmental impacts. The project will displace some families and businesses but aims to reduce air pollution and improve traffic safety. The freeway will also feature exclusive transit lanes and landscaping to integrate with the community.
Seattle, Washington, from 36th Avenue Southwest to Interstate 5
City of Seattle, Department of Engineering

The West Seattle Freeway project was proposed to enhance transportation efficiency and support industrial growth in the region. While the project aimed to reduce air pollution and improve traffic safety, it faced public pushback due to concerns about displacement of families and businesses. Ultimately, the project moved forward, balancing infrastructure needs with community impacts.

"Residents of West Seattle have been attracted there by the natural beauty of the area and by the nearby employment opportunities of the Duwamish Valley. Through historic attachment, they consider themselves to be members of a separate community within the City of Seattle." — West Seattle Residential Community Section

"Much of the area has been zoned for industrial use and as a result of the industrial influence the Riverside residential area has been declining for a number of years rather than prospering." — Conditions Relating to the Project
